items.title: Expectation and variance
items.description: Expected value E[X] and variance Var(X) are computed from the PMF or PDF introduced here; they summarize where a random variable centers and how spread out it is.

items.title: Common distributions
items.description: Named distributions (Bernoulli, Binomial, Poisson, Gaussian, etc.) are specific random variables with closed-form PMFs, PDFs and CDFs built on these definitions.

items.title: Cross-entropy loss
items.description: Cross-entropy compares a predicted PMF over class labels to the true label distribution—both are distributions of discrete random variables.

items.title: Softmax activation
items.description: Softmax converts logits into a valid categorical PMF over class indices or vocabulary tokens—a discrete random variable's predicted distribution.

items.title: Generative models
items.description: Generative models learn joint or conditional distributions over data; understanding PMF/PDF/CDF clarifies likelihood, sampling, and density estimation.

items.title: Logistic regression outputs
items.description: Logistic regression parameterizes P(Y = 1) for a Bernoulli random variable Y—the PMF of a binary label.
